summary: Frontend-only testing of 5 download buttons after refactor from <a href> to fetch+blob downloadFile(). All 5 buttons trigger correct browser download events with correct suggested filenames and file sizes. Backend export endpoints verified via curl (200 OK, correct content-types). XML content verified to contain '[ШТАТНИЙ]' prefix on Material resources (20 occurrences). Static deploy zip served via React dev server's public/files/ folder works through ingress.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor (non-blocking): showToast() in App.js does not clear the previous setTimeout when a new toast replaces an old one. If a user clicks multiple download buttons rapidly (<2.5s apart), the earliest timeout will hide the latest toast prematurely. Suggest storing the timeout id in a ref and clearing it on each new showToast call.
App.js is now ~793 lines — close to the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting StructureTab, EquipmentEditor, InteractionsTab, SummaryTab into separate files for maintainability.

Inconsistency: 4 download buttons use fetch+blob (uniform UX, shows 'Завантажено:' toast and ⏳ spinner) while dl-deploy uses native <a href download>. dl-deploy will NOT show toast, will NOT show spinner, and on download error (e.g. 404) will leave user on a broken page instead of giving a friendly error. Recommend converting dl-deploy to also use downloadFile() for consistency, OR document why it differs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 download buttons (dl-org, dl-cmd, dl-int, dl-zip, dl-deploy) work end-to-end via Playwright download event capture. fetch+blob refactor fixed the original issue. Public URL: https://bcs-roster-control.preview.emergentagent.com. Note: /files/* path is served by the React dev server (public/files/), NOT by FastAPI — be aware if production build/deployment changes path routing.
